rationale: The page provides a detailed comparison between the Booker Prize and the International Booker Prize, including their history, criteria, prize money distribution, and notable Indian winners. This is highly relevant for general knowledge and culture-related exams.
cleanedText: Booker Prize vs. International Booker Prize. Booker Prize (formerly Man Booker Prize): Started: 1969. For Best Novel in English. Originally written in English. Authors of ANY Nationality. Published in UK/Ireland. Prize: £50,000 - Author Only. Indian Winners (Booker Prize): Arundhati Roy - "The God of Small Things" (1997); Aravind Adiga - "The White Tiger" (2008); Salman Rushdie - "Midnight's Children" (1981). International Booker Prize: Started: 2005 (Annual from 2016). For Best Translated Fiction. ANY Language, Translated to English. Author & Translator BOTH Win. Prize: £50,000 Split 50-50!. Indian Connection (International Booker Prize): Geetanjali Shree - "Tomb of Sand" (2022); Trans: Daisy Rockwell; 1st Hindi Novel to Win!. Quick Comparison: [Booker Prize: English, 1969, £50K - Only Author, Arundhati Roy] [Intl. Booker Prize: Translated, 2005, £50K - 50:50 Split (Author & Translator), Geetanjali Shree]. Summary: Booker Prize - Original English Novel; Intl. Booker Prize - Translated Fiction!
